contained: Feature goal: add warning when both pytest.ini and pyproject.toml exist
Behavior: no functional change, warning only
User notification: pytest.ini takes precedence over pyproject.toml
Checklist completion: tests added, changelog created, docs updated
missing: Warning message text (exact wording not specified)
Warning severity/level (is it a WARNING, INFO, or other log level?)
When the warning is triggered (detection logic - both files present at startup? on config load? specific condition?)
Error handling if file reading fails
Whether warning appears once or multiple times
Whether warning blocks execution or is informational only
Module/file paths where changes are made
Whether this applies to all pytest invocations or specific conditions
Edge cases (symlinks, permissions, nested configs, pytest discovery process details)
